seed_problem: Weighted non-crossing base pairing maximizing stacking energy (Nussinov-style, weighted/constrained open variant); baseline = greedy pairing.
mutation: composite: keep the non-crossing base-pairing goal but (1) make the objective cooperative/non-linear (helix stacking bonus, so lone pairs are near-worthless) and (2) couple pair selection to a second aptamer-pocket objective (unpaired loop positions gain value only when enclosed by a real helix). Best strategy must balance building high-weight helices against enclosing high-value pockets.
title: Cooperative Ribozyme Folding with Aptamer Pockets
strategies: reference greedy nested matching: parenthesis-style stack matching of nearest complementary bases (the calibration baseline)
weight-greedy: enumerate all valid base pairs, add highest-weight-first if non-crossing and positions free (packs C-G pairs but ignores stacking and pockets)

helix-and-pocket heuristic: enumerate maximal complementary stems, score each by stacking energy plus enclosed pocket value, greedily select a nested compatible set, then fill leftovers with high-weight pairs
DP / local search over nested structures balancing helix stacking against pocket enclosure
